title: Power BI - Data Sources, Connectivity, And Refresh Conventions
applies_to: Power BI scheduled refresh and data currency planning

confirmed_facts.fact: Power BI can connect to many data sources, including files, databases, Azure data services, online services, and other sources documented in the data-sources catalog.
Power BI supports different connectivity modes and source-specific requirements, and not every data source supports every capability or refresh pattern in the same way.
Scheduled refresh is configured in the Power BI service for imported semantic models and depends on source connectivity, credentials, and gateway configuration when required.
If a semantic model remains unused for two months, Power BI pauses scheduled refresh, and it resumes when the semantic model is viewed again.
Scheduled refresh can fail because of credential issues, expired tokens or passwords, missing gateway configuration, source unavailability, or unsupported source scenarios.

implementation_notes: Treat refresh strategy as part of report design, not as an afterthought. A dashboard is only as useful as the semantic model refresh behind it.
Document the data source type, credential type, gateway requirement, and expected refresh cadence for every production report.
When prototyping with samples, be explicit about whether the final solution will use imported data, live connectivity, or another source pattern because this changes refresh design.
If freshness matters, add operational guidance for refresh monitoring, failure notification, and token or credential renewal.
caveats: The data-sources catalog is broad and source-specific. Production implementations must re-check the exact source documentation for the chosen connector or source.
Refresh behavior depends on licensing, capacity, source type, and gateway placement.
A report design can be visually correct while still being operationally weak if refresh prerequisites are not met.
